commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes shared message-presentation fallback text to render typed command button actions, adds Feishu document-comment guidance for command fallbacks, updates focused tests, and documents fallback visibility/privacy.

PR surface: Source +34, Tests +36, Docs +20. Total +90 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main routes Feishu document-comment interactive payloads through text fallback, and the adjacent test expects an approval command value to degrade to label-only text; I did not execute tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A single narrow PR-line blocker remains in the Feishu guidance predicate and tests, so automation can attempt the repair without a product decision.

target.title: fix(feishu): preserve button command values in fallback text and add Feishu comment guidance with callback privacy
target.body:
## Root Cause

When Feishu chat or document comments strip interactive blocks (buttons, cards), the fallback text rendered by `renderMessagePresentationFallbackText` only preserved URL-type button targets. Command buttons (with `.value` or `.action.command` / `.action.type === "callback"`) were silently dropped — users saw a bare button label like `- Approve` with no way to act on it. On Feishu comment threads, no guidance was offered to explain that interactive buttons are unsupported and cannot be used.

The fix also originally exposed legacy opaque `value` fields as visible text, which crossed the callback privacy boundary. A follow-up review fix tightened this to only render explicit typed `action.type === "command"` values.

## Summary

- Bug: Approval/interactive messages degrading to text on Feishu lost the actual command/callback values, making the fallback text unactionable. Users had no way to know what command to type manually.
- What this PR changes:
  - `src/interactive/payload.ts`: `renderMessagePresentationFallbackText` now calls `resolveMessagePresentationControlValue` for each button to extract its command value when no URL is present and `action.type === "command"`. These values are rendered as inline code (e.g., `- Deny: \`/approve req_1 deny\``) so the user can copy and type them manually. Legacy opaque `value` fields and `action.type === "callback"` values remain private — only the button label is shown.
  - `extensions/feishu/src/outbound.ts`: When interactive content with command-type actions is detected in a Feishu comment thread, appends a user-friendly guidance line: `> Interactive buttons are unavailable in Feishu document comments. You can type the command shown above manually.` The guidance is gated on actual `action.type === "command"` buttons — label-only or callback-only blocks do not trigger it.
  - Test updates across `payload.test.ts` and `outbound.test.ts` to assert the new fallback format.
  - Added JSDoc to `renderMessagePresentationFallbackText` documenting the typed command fallback semantics for SDK consumers.
  - Updated `docs/plugins/message-presentation.md` with a "Button value fallback visibility" section documenting the command/callback/legacy value privacy contract for plugin authors.
- Fixes #69754

## Review Fixes

- **P1 — callback privacy**: `action.type === "callback"` values are no longer rendered in fallback text. These are opaque channel/plugin data, not manual commands. Additionally, legacy `.value` fields (without a typed `action`) are also kept private — only explicit `action.type === "command"` values are rendered as copyable text. This addresses the `merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary` concern flagged by ClawSweeper.
- **P2 — gate Feishu guidance on command action**: Feishu comment thread guidance now checks for actual `action.type === "command"` buttons, instead of the broader `isMessagePresentationInteractiveBlock` which matched any buttons/selects blocks. This ensures the "You can type the command shown above manually" text only appears when a copyable command is actually rendered in the fallback text — avoiding misleading guidance on label-only fallbacks (callback, legacy value, or URL-only buttons).
- **P3 — document command/callback fallback visibility**: Added a "Button value fallback visibility" subsection under "Degradation rules" in `docs/plugins/message-presentation.md` documenting which button value types are rendered as copyable text (`action.type === "command"`) vs. kept private (callback, legacy value, select option values). Channel adapters that add manual-command guidance are advised to derive the command-present check from the same source the fallback renderer uses.

## Verification

- `pnpm build` — build clean
- `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts src/interactive/payload.test.ts` — all tests passed
- `pnpm check:test-types` — clean, 0 errors
- `pnpm lint:changed` — lint clean
- CI: [pending](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions) — no CI failures at last check

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Approval/interactive messages degrading to text on Feishu lost command/callback button values; Feishu comment threads lacked guidance that interactive buttons are unavailable. Typed `action.type === "command"` values are rendered as copyable fallback text, while legacy opaque `value` and `action.type === "callback"` data stay private (label-only).

Real environment tested: Linux, Node v24.13.1, branch `fix/issue-69754-feishu-approval-fallback`, commits spanning `62a5c516e6` through `7b46a710cf`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# 1. Build the project binary from current branch
pnpm build

# 2. Run core unit tests covering the fallback renderer
pnpm test src/interactive/payload.test.ts

# 3. Run changed surface tests for Feishu
pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

**Binary proof at 62a5c516e6 (initial implementation — command rendering verified):**
```console
$ node /tmp/proof-94385.mjs
OpenClaw real behavior proof — PR #94385
Node: v24.13.1, Platform: linux
Date: 2026-06-18T03:50:08.332Z
========================================================================

Input: approval presentation with 5 button variants

Button analysis (resolveMessagePresentationControlValue):
  Approve: controlValue="/approve req_1 allow-once"
  Deny: controlValue="/approve req_1 deny"
  Comment: controlValue="comment_123"
  Docs: controlValue=undefined
  Disabled: controlValue=undefined

Output (renderMessagePresentationFallbackText):
- Approve: `/approve req_1 allow-once`
- Deny: `/approve req_1 deny`
- Comment: `comment_123`
- Docs: https://example.com/docs
- Disabled

Behavior change:
  OLD (before fix):           NEW (after fix):
  - Approve                           - Approve: `/approve req_1 allow-once`  ← command/callback values added
  - Deny                              - Deny: `/approve req_1 deny`  ← command/callback values added
  - Comment                           - Comment: `comment_123`  ← command/callback values added
  - Docs: https://example.com/docs    - Docs: https://example.com/docs
  - Disabled                          - Disabled

Feishu comment delivery output with guidance:
- Approve: `/approve req_1 allow-once`
- Deny: `/approve req_1 deny`
- Comment: `comment_123`
- Docs: https://example.com/docs
- Disabled

> Interactive buttons are unavailable in Feishu document comments. You can type the command shown above manually.
```

**Updated binary proof at 344b3c7 (privacy tightening — legacy value no longer exposed):**
```console
$ pnpm test src/interactive/payload.test.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  17 passed (17)
# Legacy value button "Approve" now renders as "- Approve" (label-only)
# Typed command button "Deny" still renders as "- Deny: `/approve req_1 deny`"

$ pnpm test:changed
# core: 17/17 · feishu: 42/42 · telegram: 78/78
# Total: 137 tests passed across 3 shards

$ pnpm openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (344b3c7)

$ git rev-parse --short HEAD
344b3c76d9
# ✅ Binary matches current HEAD
```

**Updated guidance-gate proof at 565b020 (command-action gate):**
```console
$ node scripts/verify-feishu-fallback-proof.mjs
============================================================
Real behavior proof: Feishu fallback text with command-only guidance gate
============================================================
--- Behavior change ---
OLD (before fix):
  Feishu guidance shown for ANY interactive block
  => `- Approve` shown (no command value, callback kept private)
  => Guidance: 'You can type the command shown above manually'
  => User told to type a command that is NOT visible -- MISLEADING

NEW (after fix):
  Feishu guidance shown ONLY when command-type action exists
  => `- Approve: /approve req_1` (command value visible and copyable)
  => `- Ignore` (callback value kept private)
  => `- Docs: https://example.com/docs` (URL shown)
  => Guidance: 'You can type the command shown above manually' (only when hasCommandAction=true)

Rendered fallback text:
Review this

- Approve: `/approve req_1`
- Ignore
- Docs: https://example.com/docs

> Interactive buttons are unavailable in Feishu document comments. You can type the command shown above manually.

--- Verification ---
  Command value in fallback text: ✓ YES
  Callback value kept private:   ✓ PRIVATE
  Guidance shown:                ✓ YES
  Guidance correctly gated:      ✓ PASS
Overall: ✓ PASS
```

**Telegram Desktop proof (Mantis):**
- Mantis captured native Telegram Desktop before/after GIFs showing the command button fallback text.
- Artifact: [run 27890243350](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27890243350/artifacts/7771189492)

Observed result after fix: The fallback renderer correctly renders typed `action.type === "command"` values as copyable inline code (e.g., `- Deny: \`/approve req_1 deny\``), while legacy opaque `value` fields and `action.type === "callback"` data remain private (label-only, e.g., `- Approve` without value). The Feishu guidance gate is narrowed to only show when a command action is actually present — eliminating misleading instructions on label-only fallbacks. All relevant tests pass.

What was not tested: Live Feishu E2E roundtrip (requires real Feishu bot credentials and Crabbox environment). The fallback text rendering and guidance gate are validated against unit tests, standalone proof output, and Telegram Desktop visual proof captured by Mantis.
target.author: xialonglee

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
